description_long: This is a dark, angular armored combat vehicle resembling a World War II-era German tank, featuring a low-profile turret and a wide, flat hull. The exterior is covered in faceted armor plates, with visible sloping surfaces designed to deflect projectiles. The vehicle is equipped with continuous metal tracks for all-terrain mobility, and the sides display a cross insignia and the number '13' in white. The turret appears to have a main gun barrel and possibly a coaxial secondary weapon, both mounted forward. The surface is finished in a dark, geometric camouflage pattern, giving it a stealthy appearance. The overall build suggests heavy armor and significant mass, with functional hatches and access points implied by the geometry.
description: A dark, angular armored tank with sloped armor, a low turret, and visible German-style markings. It features metal tracks and a geometric camouflage pattern.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tank, the cross insignia, number '13', and the main gun barrel extending from the turret.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sloped frontal armor, narrow profile, and the main gun barrel centered on the turret.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with visible tracks, cross insignia, and turret details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back armor, engine deck, and the rear section of the tracks, with no visible weapon mounts.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One main gun barrel mounted on the turret front; possible coaxial secondary weapon also on the turret.
attribute_armor_plating: Full body coverage with thick, sloped armor plating; faceted surfaces for deflection; no visible modular or reactive panels.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Dark geometric camouflage pattern with black and gray angular shapes.
attribute_military_markings: White cross insignia and the number '13' on the turret sides.
